agent_memory.purpose: Nuanced concepts for a portfolio construction/stock picking agent.

sections.context: Tissue/towel & diaper-adjacent SKUs swing with pulp cycles; detergent/cleaners ride resin & surfactant chains (HDPE/PP, LAB/LAS, EO/PO). These indices front-run gross margin inflections and COGS outlooks.
sections.investment_insight: Build a COGS watchlist and map category exposure by SKU. Use pulp and plastics/surfactant prints to anticipate pricing needs and GM turns.

sections.context: NY 1,4-dioxane caps forced reformulations across detergents/cleaners; EU microplastics restriction (including encapsulated fragrances) is phasing in with multi-year transitions; EPA Safer Choice and disinfectant List N claims create label moats and ongoing compliance costs.
sections.investment_insight: Track compliance roadmaps and reformulation milestones; lapses can hit efficacy perception, velocity, and margins.
metrics_raw: EU microplastics restriction with phased timelines (encapsulated fragrances impacted)
EPA Safer Choice and List N claims require maintenance and retesting

sections.context: Private label penetration and share gains are highest in paper/tissue and commodity cleaners; enzymatic detergents remain more brand-defensible.
sections.investment_insight: Names over-indexed to commodity-like subcategories face greater price/mix pressure in downturns and value channels.
metrics_raw: High PL penetration: paper/tissue, commodity cleaners

sections.context: Costco's markup cap compresses gross margin headroom and accelerates trade-down to Kirkland; Walmart OTIF and big-box compliance penalties act like a hidden tax on supply chains.
sections.investment_insight: Scrutinize channel mix (club/value) and compliance costs; check whether scale offsets exist.
metrics_raw: Walmart OTIF penalties impact when service levels slip

sections.topic: Digital Shelf as a Leading Indicator
sections.context: %ACV/TDP + velocity are gold standard offline; online, share of search (page-1), content quality (ratings/Q&A/pack images), and in-stock rates predict takeaway on Amazon/Instacart.
sections.investment_insight: Monitor digital-shelf KPIs weekly to front-run scanner data and detect early momentum or deterioration.
metrics_raw: Rating count & score, Q&A health, image compliance

sections.context: Auto-replenishment (e.g., Subscribe & Save) smooths volume and raises switching costs in detergents and other consumables; category growth increasingly tracks digital grocery penetration.
sections.investment_insight: Favor portfolios with high recurring reorder penetration and rising subscription share.

sections.context: Unit economics are managed via sizes/formats (mega vs concentrated). Regulatory and media scrutiny of size changes is rising; shrinkflation bite is notably higher in household paper.
sections.investment_insight: Differentiate genuine mix upgrades from stealth downsizing; track consumer sentiment and complaint velocity.
metrics_raw: Household paper most exposed to shrinkflation optics

sections.topic: Claimability as a Moat in Disinfecting
sections.context: "Kills 99.9%" and residual-efficacy claims are tightly regulated; maintaining master registrations and updated efficacy protocols is ongoing and costly.
sections.investment_insight: Treat claim maintenance like capex; lapses can erase perceived product advantage and shelf velocity.
metrics_raw: Residual efficacy claim status and retest cadence

sections.context: Category captains influence assortment/planograms, entrenching leaders but drawing scrutiny; loss of captaincy often coincides with share step-downs.
sections.investment_insight: Track captaincy status changes as a leading indicator for share shifts.

sections.context: EDLP compresses promo spikes; hi-lo banners reward feature/display. Separate base vs incremental sales—weak brands over-index to promo-driven lifts with poor repeat.
sections.investment_insight: Favor brands with healthy base velocity and efficient trade spend; penalize promo-dependent stories.

sections.context: Detergent enzyme innovation (cold-water performance, stain removal) is paced by specialty suppliers; availability/pricing shifts can re-order value tiers.
sections.investment_insight: Watch supplier disclosures and input availability; chemistry roadmaps can signal future brand hierarchies.

sections.context: Cyberattacks and plant outages in automated fill/pack networks ripple through category share and promo calendars for multiple quarters.
sections.investment_insight: Screen for redundancy, dual sourcing, and inventory visibility; resilience reduces earnings volatility.

sections.topic: Packaging EPR Fees = Structural COGS Headwind
sections.context: California SB 54 and similar EPR laws impose eco-modulated fees by material, pushing brands toward PCR content and lighter packaging over time.
sections.investment_insight: Model rising packaging fees and capex for material shifts (PCR adoption, lightweighting) into long-range COGS.

sections.topic: Winning vs Private Label: Scent + Efficacy + Convenience
sections.context: Where PL pressures are highest, brands must differentiate on scent systems (encapsulation), dosing convenience (pods, sprays), and verified efficacy; digital reviews amplify perception gaps.
sections.investment_insight: Track review velocity/content and sensory claims alongside ACV/TDP to judge pricing power durability.

sections.context: A consistent KPI set enables cross-name comparability and earlier reads on inflections across household products.
sections.investment_insight: Use this toolkit to structure dashboards and quarterly checklists; tie KPIs to P&L sensitivity by subcategory.

sections.topic: Value-Driven, Fundamentals Screen (How to Use This)
sections.context: Guidance for applying the above dynamics to a value-first stock-picking process within Household Products.
sections.investment_insight: Prefer portfolios with defensible categories (lower PL risk), strong TDP/velocity, stable or improving digital-shelf share, demonstrated compliance/reg-ready formulas, and diversified input exposure (or explicit hedging). Haircut/avoid names leaning on promo to hit volume, outsized exposure to pulp/resin without pricing power, lagging reformulation roadmaps (1,4-dioxane/microplastics), or channel mixes over-indexed to club/value banners without scale offsets.
